text: The surgeon elevates the greater omentum to expose the small bowel, revealing the area of obstruction caused by the gallstone. Sutures are placed, and a longitudinal enterotomy is performed to retrieve the stone. The enterotomy is then closed transversely to prevent stricturing, and the remainder of the small intestine is examined for additional stones.

The surgeon focuses on the right upper quadrant, where an extensive inflammatory response around the gallbladder is visible. The surrounding omentum is carefully dissected to expose the gallbladder fundus. A cholecystotomy is performed, and gallstones are retrieved from the gallbladder lumen. The cholecystoduodenal fistula is clearly visible, with no residual stones present. The area is irrigated, and the gallbladder is closed with interrupted figure-of-eight silk sutures.
